steps.title: Identify the key context
steps.content: Reed argues historians present leaders as "the major actors" and workers as "nearly devoid of agency." The blank must describe what historians have done to ordinary workers — treating them as peripheral or unimportant.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — epitomize: wrong direction — to epitomize means to serve as a perfect example of something, which would elevate workers, not minimize them. ✗
  • Choice B — marginalize: fits precisely — to marginalize means to treat as unimportant or peripheral, matching "nearly devoid of agency" and the contrast with leaders as "major actors." ✓
  • Choice C — extol: wrong direction — to extol means to praise highly, which contradicts the passage's point that historians have downplayed workers' importance. ✗
  • Choice D — substantiate: wrong meaning — to substantiate means to verify with evidence; the passage implies historians have overlooked evidence of workers' influence, not confirmed it. ✗
That matches Choice B — marginalize.
